paper_title: Bioinformatic and ATR-FTIR Analysis of Ambrosia Pollen

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of 454-read sets to assemble and quantify expression levels of Ambrosia transcripts, identify orthologous gene pairs, and perform sequence similarity searches.

steps.description: The fraction of small 454-reads (24%) was removed and excluded from the assembly process.
For the assemblies, the Newbler v.2.5.3 transcriptome assembly (-cDNA option) and default parameters were used.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble reads using Newbler v.2.5.3 with cDNA option

steps.description: The available 454-reads of the individual samples were aligned to the assembled isotigs by using vmatch v2.1.7 (-l 40 -e 1 -identity 98).
steps.searchable_summary: align reads to isotigs using vmatch

steps.description: Returned alignments were filtered and for each read only alignments with maximum e-value were considered for further analysis.
Transcript expression levels were quantified in reads per kilobase of exon model per million mapped reads (RPKM).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate RPKM for transcript expression levels

steps.description: Putative orthologous gene pairs between assembled Ambrosia genes and Arabidopsis (TAIR10) were identified based on a two level homology search.
steps.searchable_summary: identify orthologous gene pairs using BLASTP

steps.description: Sequence similarity searches against the NCBI nonredundant protein database and known allergen sets were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequence similarity search using BLASTP and BLASTN

methods.description: This method involves the use of ATR-FTIR spectroscopy to analyze the chemical composition of Ambrosia pollen treated with elevated ozone compared to control samples.

steps.description: The ATR-FTIR spectra of Ambrosia pollen treated with elevated ozone compared to the control are shown in Figure 2.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ATR-FTIR spectra of pollen samples

steps.description: The two strong absorption bands located at ,2850 and ,2920 cm 21 originate from the asymmetric and symmetric stretching vibrations of the methyl and methylene groups.
steps.searchable_summary: identify absorption bands for methyl and methylene groups

steps.description: The minor IR absorption band recorded at ,1470 cm 21 may be assigned to methylene deformation vibrations.
steps.searchable_summary: assign IR absorption band to methylene deformation

steps.description: Absorptions recorded between 1660-1500 cm 21 corresponded to the aromatic domain of the samples.
The proteins' amid I absorption was recorded between 1680-1620 cm 21.
steps.searchable_summary: record amid I absorption for proteins

steps.description: A set of different absorption bands were recorded in the 1200-950 cm 21 region.
steps.searchable_summary: record absorption bands in 1200-950 cm 21 region

steps.description: The absorption bands at ,1770 and ,1740 cm 21 may originate from the C = O stretching vibration of the carbonyl group.
